calendar.matters:
PLAINTIFF JEREMY RICARD'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6. (pursuant to Court Order filed 1/27/26)

Off Calendar Mar-25-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JEREMY RICARD'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6.

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: motion to enforce settlement agreement against defendant Velo Labs, Inc. is granted. Judgment shall enter against this defendant in accordance with the terms of the settlement agreement attached as Exhibit A to the November 12, 2025 Ruddell Declaration. Ricard shall submit a proposed judgment.

The motion is denied without prejudice as to individual defendant Jack Al-Kahwati in light of the bankruptcy stay. Plaintiff may refile when the stay is lifted.

Al-Kahwati submitted a separate brief asking to delay entry of judgment against Velo Labs in light of the fact that he is an owner of this corporation. This request is denied; the automatic stay extends only to the debtor who is a party to bankruptcy proceedings and not to separate entities owned by the debtor. (See, e.g., New Orleans Gas & Elec. Lights, Inc. v. Me'lange Joli, Inc. (M.D. La., July 8, 2011, No. CIV.A. 11-194-JJB-CN) 2011 WL 2680578, at *2 ["a corporation which is wholly owned by a bankruptcy debtor is considered a separate and distinct legal entity, and, as such, actions against the corporation are not subject to the stay"]; In re Calhoun (Bankr. N.D. Iowa 2004) 312 B.R. 380, 384 ["The automatic stay does not stay actions against separate entities associated with the debtor."].)

Proposed order submitted. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
